system.effect: The character must pass a Willpower test modified by the Fear level or suffer its effects. Fear (1): −10 WP test or suffer 1 level of Fatigue. Fear (2): −20 WP test or flee for 1 Round and gain 1d5 Insanity Points. Fear (3): −30 WP test or flee for 1d5 Rounds and gain 1d10 Insanity Points. Fear (4): automatic flee for 1d10 Rounds and gain 1d10+5 Insanity Points.
system.removal: Fear effects end after their specified duration. Some talents (such as Nerves of Steel or Fearless) allow resistance or immunity.

description.value:
The character is confronted by something terrifying. Fear has four levels (1–4), each requiring a Willpower test with increasing penalties:

  • Fear (1): Willpower test at −10. Failure causes 1 level of Fatigue.
  • Fear (2): Willpower test at −20. Failure causes the character to flee for 1 Round and gain 1d5 Insanity Points.
  • Fear (3): Willpower test at −30. Failure causes the character to flee for 1d5 Rounds and gain 1d10 Insanity Points.
  • Fear (4): No test permitted. The character flees for 1d10 Rounds and gains 1d10+5 Insanity Points.

system.notes: Set stacks to the Fear level (1-4). Characters with the Fearless talent are immune to Fear up to a certain level.
